words.example: Butter is made from raw cow's milk.
Milk turns into a block of solid butter.
The machine separates the cream from the milk.
The cream is beaten in a churner.
Churning breaks down the fat droplets.
Churning breaks down the thin covering.
Each tiny fat droplet has a thin cover.

params.title: How Butter Is Made
params.meaning: 버터가 만들어지는 진짜 과정을 따라가는 이야기예요. 색칠된 단어를 누르면 설명을 듣고, 🎙로 선생님과 이야기도 나눠보세요.

wordExplanations.raw: Raw means not cooked. Butter is made from raw cow's milk.
wordExplanations.solid: Solid means hard and firm, with a clear shape. Butter is solid. Milk is not.
wordExplanations.separate: Separate means to take things apart, so they are not together anymore.
wordExplanations.beat: Beat means to hit something many, many times.
wordExplanations.churn: Churn means to beat or shake cream until it becomes butter.
wordExplanations.break down: Break down means to break something into small pieces.
wordExplanations.fat: Fat is the white or yellow oily part of food. Butter is mostly fat.
wordExplanations.droplet: A droplet is a very, very small drop of liquid.

voiceTalks.raw: Target word: raw (= not cooked). In the story, butter is made from raw cow's milk. Ask ONE easy question, e.g.: Is raw food cooked? or: What raw food do you know?
voiceTalks.solid: Target word: solid (= hard and firm, with a clear shape). In the story, milk turns into a block of solid butter. Ask ONE easy question, e.g.: Is milk solid? Is ice solid?
voiceTalks.separate: Target word: separate (= to take things apart). In the story, a separator separates the fatty cream from the milk. Ask ONE easy question, e.g.: What does the separator separate?
voiceTalks.beat: Target word: beat (= to hit many times). In the story, the cream is beaten in a churner. Ask this book question: What is the closest meaning of beat - to boil, or to hit?
voiceTalks.churn: Target word: churn (= to beat or shake cream into butter). Ask ONE easy question, e.g.: What do we get when we churn cream?
voiceTalks.break down: Target phrase: break down (= to break into small pieces). In the story, churning breaks down the thin cover of each fat droplet. Ask ONE easy question, e.g.: What breaks down the covers of the droplets?
voiceTalks.fat: Target word: fat (= the oily part of food). In the story, fat droplets join together and become butter. Ask ONE easy question, e.g.: Which foods have a lot of fat?
voiceTalks.droplet: Target word: droplet (= a very small drop of liquid). Ask ONE easy question, e.g.: Is a droplet big or small? Where can you see droplets?

panels.caption: We love butter on warm toast!
Butter starts as raw cow's milk.
A truck carries the fresh milk to a factory.
A separator separates the fatty cream from the milk.
The cream is heated to kill the germs.
A churner beats the cream. Churn, churn, churn!
Churning breaks down the cover of each tiny fat droplet.
Now it is a block of solid butter. Yum!
